content:
# Exodus Chapter 36 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 107–108; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 79–80 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_036_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_036_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_036_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_036_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — SURPLUS AND STRUCTURE:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 36 across pages 107–108, continuing directly from the appointment of Bezalel and Oholiab. This chapter divides into two distinct movements: the astonishing surplus of offerings (vv. 1–7), where the people must be restrained from giving, and the meticulous construction of the tabernacle's fabric and frame (vv. 8–38). The construction account largely mirrors the instructions of chapter 26, but now shifts from command to execution — from "you shall make" to "he made." The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 79–80) dramatically rearranges this material: what MT has as Exodus 36 is scattered across LXX Exodus 36–37 (corresponding roughly to MT's chapter 26 instructions), with the surplus-offering narrative placed differently. The LXX headings on the Vaticanus pages visible in the manuscript read CAP. XXVI and CAP. XXVII, confirming the well-known LXX reordering of the tabernacle construction chapters.

---

## The Craftsmen Begin and the Surplus (vv. 1–7)

**1** And Bezalel and Oholiab shall work [ve-asah], and every man wise of heart [ve-khol ish khakham-lev] in whom YHWH has put [natan] wisdom [khokhmah] and understanding [u-tvunah] in them, to know [la-da'at] to do [la'asot] all the work of the service [et-kol-melekhet avodat] of the holy place [ha-qodesh], according to all that [le-khol asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah].

> **Key terms:** לָדַעַת לַעֲשֹׂת (la-da'at la'asot) = "to know to do" — knowledge and action are inseparable; wisdom is expressed through making. **LXX:** rearranges this material; the craftsmen's commission appears in a different sequence in Vaticanus.

**2** And Moses called [va-yiqra] to Bezalel and to Oholiab and to every man wise of heart [khakham-lev] in whose heart [be-libbo] YHWH had put [natan] wisdom [khokhmah] — everyone whose heart lifted him [kol asher nesa'o libbo] to draw near [le-qorvah] to the work [el-ha-melakhah] to do it [la'asot otah].

> **Key terms:** לְקָרְבָה (le-qorvah) = "to draw near/approach" — from קרב; the craftsmen are drawn to the sacred work as one draws near to worship. נְשָׂאוֹ לִבּוֹ (nesa'o libbo) = "his heart lifted him" — repeated from 35:21; the heart as agent of vocation. **LXX:** the calling of craftsmen is placed differently in Vaticanus.

**3** And they took [va-yiqkhu] from before Moses [mi-lifnei Mosheh] all the contribution [et-kol-ha-terumah] which the Sons of Israel had brought [hevi'u] for the work of the service [li-mlekhet avodat] of the holy place [ha-qodesh] to do it [la'asot otah]. And they — they kept bringing [hevi'u] to him [elav] still [od] a freewill-offering [nedavah] morning by morning [ba-boqer ba-boqer].

> **Key terms:** בַּבֹּקֶר בַּבֹּקֶר (ba-boqer ba-boqer) = "in the morning in the morning" — distributive repetition: each and every morning, without fail. נְדָבָה (nedavah) = "freewill-offering" — the surplus keeps arriving unbidden. **LXX:** τὸ πρωὶ πρωί ("early, early"), preserving the doubling.

**4** And all the wise ones [kol-ha-khakhamim] came — the ones doing [ha-osim] all the work of the holy place [et kol-melekhet ha-qodesh] — each man from his work [ish-ish mi-melakhto] which they [asher-hemah] were doing [osim] —

> **Key terms:** אִישׁ אִישׁ (ish-ish) = "each man, each man" — emphatic distributive: every single one, individually. **LXX:** ἕκαστος ("each one"), losing the repetition.

**5** and they said [va-yomeru] to Moses, saying [lemor], "The people [ha-am] are bringing more [marbim le-havi] than enough [mi-dei] for the service [ha-avodah] of the work [la-melakhah] which YHWH commanded [tsivvah] to do [la'asot] it."

> **Key terms:** מַרְבִּים לְהָבִיא מִדֵּי (marbim le-havi mi-dei) = "are multiplying to bring more than enough" — Hiphil participle of רבה; the generosity exceeds the need. This is the only place in Torah where too much is given. **LXX:** Vaticanus places this episode differently in its rearranged sequence.

**6** And Moses commanded [va-yetsav], and they caused a voice to pass [va-ya'aviru qol] through the camp [ba-makhaneh], saying [lemor], "Let no man or woman [al-ya'asu od melakhah] do [ish ve-ishah] any more work [od melakhah] for the contribution of the holy place [li-trumat ha-qodesh]." And the people were restrained [va-yikkale ha-am] from bringing [me-havi].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעֲבִירוּ קוֹל (va-ya'aviru qol) = "and they caused a voice to pass" — Hiphil of עבר; a proclamation reverberates through the camp. וַיִּכָּלֵא (va-yikkale) = "and were restrained" — Niphal of כלא; the people had to be held back from generosity. **LXX:** Vaticanus has a briefer version of this restraining notice.

**7** And the work [ve-ha-melakhah] was enough [dayam] for them — for all the work [le-khol ha-melakhah] — to do it [la'asot otah], and more [ve-hoter] — (surplus).

> **Key terms:** דַיָּם (dayam) = "sufficient/enough for them." וְהוֹתֵר (ve-hoter) = "and surplus/remainder" — Hiphil participle of יתר; not merely sufficient but overflowing. The verse is strikingly terse — a single sentence of abundance. **LXX:** καὶ ἐπερίσσευσεν ("and it was left over"), a reasonable rendering.

---

## The Ten Curtains of the Tabernacle (vv. 8–13)

**8** And every wise-hearted one [kol khakham-lev] among the doers [be-osei] of the work [ha-melakhah] made [va-ya'asu] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan]: ten curtains [eser yeri'ot] of twisted fine linen [shesh moshezar] and blue [u-tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet of worm [ve-tola'at shani] — cherubim [keruvim], the work of a designer [ma'aseh khoshev], he made them [asah otam].

> **Key terms:** שֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר (shesh moshezar) = "twisted/twined fine linen" — Hophal participle of שׁזר ("to twist"); the base fabric. מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב (ma'aseh khoshev) = "work of a designer/thinker" — the highest grade of textile work, with woven-in figures. **LXX (=ch. 26 in Vaticanus):** δέκα αὐλαίας ἐκ βύσσου κεκλωσμένης ("ten curtains of spun linen"), matching MT.

**9** The length of the one curtain [orekh ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] — twenty-eight by the cubit [shemoneh ve-esrim ba-ammah], and the width [ve-rokhav] — four by the cubit [arba ba-ammah], the one curtain [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat]; one measure [middah akhat] for all the curtains [le-khol ha-yeri'ot].

> **Key terms:** מִדָּה אַחַת (middah akhat) = "one measure" — uniformity of dimension; each curtain identical. **LXX:** μέτρον τὸ αὐτό ("the same measure"), matching MT.

**10** And he joined [va-yekhabber] five of the curtains [khamesh ha-yeri'ot] one to another [akhat el-akhat], and five curtains [ve-khamesh yeri'ot] he joined [khibbar] one to another [akhat el-akhat].

> **Key terms:** וַיְחַבֵּר (va-yekhabber) = "and he joined/coupled" — Piel of חבר; the curtains are united into two large panels. **LXX:** συνέζευξεν ("he yoked together"), matching MT.

**11** And he made [va-ya'as] loops of blue [lula'ot tekhelet] on the edge [al sefat] of the one curtain [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] at the end [mi-qatseh] of the joining [ba-makhbaret]; thus [ken] he made [asah] on the edge [bi-sfat] of the outermost curtain [ha-yeri'ah ha-qitsonah] in the second joining [ba-makhberet ha-shenit].

> **Key terms:** לֻלָאֹת (lula'ot) = "loops" — from an uncertain root; the fastening mechanism. מַחְבֶּרֶת (makhberet) = "joining/set" — from חבר; where two panels meet. **LXX:** ἀγκύλας ὑακινθίνας ("hyacinthine loops"), matching MT.

**12** Fifty loops [khamishim lula'ot] he made [asah] on the one curtain [ba-yeri'ah ha-akhat], and fifty loops [va-khamishim lula'ot] he made [asah] on the end of the curtain [bi-qtseh ha-yeri'ah] which is in the second joining [asher ba-makhberet ha-shenit] — the loops receiving [maqbilot] one to another [ha-lula'ot akhat el-akhat].

> **Key terms:** מַקְבִּילֹת (maqbilot) = "receiving/corresponding" — Hiphil participle of קבל; the loops face each other in exact alignment. **LXX:** ἀντιπίπτουσαι ("falling opposite"), matching MT.

**13** And he made [va-ya'as] fifty clasps of gold [khamishim qarsei zahav], and he joined [va-yekhabber] the curtains [et-ha-yeri'ot] one to another [akhat el-akhat] with the clasps [ba-qerasim], and the tabernacle became [va-yehi ha-mishkan] one [ekhad].

> **Key terms:** קַרְסֵי זָהָב (qarsei zahav) = "clasps/hooks of gold" — from קרס; the golden fasteners uniting the panels. וַיְהִי... אֶחָד (va-yehi... ekhad) = "and it became one" — theological weight: the diverse curtains become a unified dwelling. **LXX:** καὶ ἐγένετο ἡ σκηνὴ μία ("and the tent became one"), matching MT precisely.

---

## The Goat-Hair Curtains (vv. 14–18)

**14** And he made [va-ya'as] curtains of goat-hair [yeri'ot izzim] for a tent [le-ohel] over the tabernacle [al-ha-mishkan] — eleven curtains [ashtei-esreh yeri'ot] he made them [asah otam].

> **Key terms:** יְרִיעֹת עִזִּים (yeri'ot izzim) = "curtains of goats" — the outer protective layer over the linen curtains. **LXX:** δέκα καὶ μία αὐλαῖαι ("eleven curtains"), matching MT.

**15** The length of the one curtain [orekh ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] — thirty by the cubit [sheloshim ba-ammah], and four cubits [ve-arba ammot] the width [rokhav] of the one curtain [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat]; one measure [middah akhat] for the eleven curtains [le-ashtei esreh yeri'ot].

> **Key terms:** Thirty cubits long, four wide — larger than the linen curtains (28 x 4), providing overhang. **LXX:** matches MT dimensions.

**16** And he joined [va-yekhabber] five of the curtains [et-khamesh ha-yeri'ot] by themselves [levad], and six of the curtains [ve-et-shesh ha-yeri'ot] by themselves [levad].

> **Key terms:** לְבָד (levad) = "separately/by themselves" — the goat-hair curtains are divided asymmetrically: 5 + 6. **LXX:** matches MT.

**17** And he made [va-ya'as] loops [lula'ot] — fifty [khamishim] — on the edge [al sfat] of the outermost curtain [ha-yeri'ah ha-qitsonah] in the joining [ba-makhbaret], and fifty loops [va-khamishim lula'ot] he made [asah] on the edge of the curtain [al sfat ha-yeri'ah] of the second joining [ha-makhberet ha-shenit].

> **Key terms:** Same loop system as the linen curtains but for the outer tent. **LXX:** matches MT.

**18** And he made [va-ya'as] clasps of bronze [qarsei nekhoshet] — fifty [khamishim] — to join [le-khabber] the tent [et-ha-ohel] to be one [lihyot ekhad].

> **Key terms:** קַרְסֵי נְחֹשֶׁת (qarsei nekhoshet) = "clasps of bronze" — bronze for the outer tent, gold for the inner; the materials grade from precious (inside) to functional (outside). לִהְיוֹת אֶחָד (lihyot ekhad) = "to be one" — again, unity as the goal. **LXX:** matches MT.

---

## The Covering and the Boards (vv. 19–30)

**19** And he made [va-ya'as] a covering [mikhseh] for the tent [la-ohel] — skins of rams [orot eilim] made-red [me'oddamim], and a covering of skins of takhash [u-mikhseh orot tekhashim] from above [mi-lema'lah].

> **Key terms:** מִכְסֶה (mikhseh) = "covering" — from כסה ("to cover"); the protective outer layers. מִלְמָעְלָה (mi-lema'lah) = "from above" — the takhash skins form the topmost, outermost covering. **LXX:** κατακάλυμμα ("covering"), matching MT.

**20** And he made [va-ya'as] the boards [et-ha-qerashim] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan] of acacia wood [atsei shittim], standing upright [omedim].

> **Key terms:** קְרָשִׁים (qerashim) = "boards/frames" — the structural skeleton of the tabernacle. עֹמְדִים (omedim) = "standing" — Qal participle of עמד; the boards stand vertically. **LXX:** στύλους ("pillars/posts") — an interpretive rendering; the Hebrew means planks or frames.

**21** Ten cubits [eser ammot] the length of the board [orekh ha-qaresh], and a cubit and a half of the cubit [ve-ammah va-khatsi ha-ammah] the width of the one board [rokhav ha-qaresh ha-ekhad].

> **Key terms:** Dimensions: 10 cubits tall by 1.5 cubits wide per board. **LXX:** matches MT dimensions.

**22** Two tenons [shtei yadot] for the one board [la-qaresh ha-ekhad], joined [meshullabbot] one to another [ishah el-akhotah]; thus [ken] he made [asah] for all the boards of the tabernacle [le-khol qarshei ha-mishkan].

> **Key terms:** יָדוֹת (yadot) = "tenons" — literally "hands"; projections that fit into the silver bases. מְשֻׁלָּבֹת (meshullabbot) = "joined/set in order" — Pual participle of שׁלב ("to join"); each tenon paired. אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ (ishah el-akhotah) = "a woman to her sister" — feminine reciprocal phrase for things paired. **LXX:** ἀγκωνίσκους ("small elbows/projections") for yadot.

**23** And he made [va-ya'as] the boards [et-ha-qerashim] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan] — twenty boards [esrim qerashim] for the south side [li-fe'at negbah teimanah] southward.

> **Key terms:** פְּאַת נֶגְבָּה תֵימָנָה (pe'at negbah teimanah) = "the side of the south, southward" — double designation reinforcing direction. **LXX:** τὸ κλίτος τὸ πρὸς νότον ("the side toward the south"), matching MT.

**24** And forty bases of silver [ve-arba'im adnei-khesef] he made [asah] under [takhat] the twenty boards [esrim ha-qerashim] — two bases [shnei adanim] under the one board [takhat ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] for its two tenons [li-shtei yadotav], and two bases [u-shnei adanim] under the one board [takhat ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] for its two tenons [li-shtei yadotav].

> **Key terms:** אֲדָנִים (adanim) = "bases/sockets" — from אדן; the silver foundation into which the boards are mortised. Forty bases for twenty boards (two per board). **LXX:** matches MT.

**25** And for the second side [u-li-tsel'a] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], for the north side [li-fe'at tsafon], he made [asah] twenty boards [esrim qerashim],

> **Key terms:** צֶלַע (tsela) = "side/rib" — the same word used for Eve's "rib" in Genesis 2:21; the tabernacle has sides as a body has ribs. **LXX:** τὸ κλίτος τὸ πρὸς βορρᾶν ("the side toward the north").

**26** and their forty bases of silver [ve-arba'im adneihem kasef] — two bases [shnei adanim] under the one board [takhat ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] and two bases [u-shnei adanim] under the one board [takhat ha-qaresh ha-ekhad].

> **Key terms:** Parallel construction to v. 24 — identical framework for north and south. **LXX:** matches MT.

**27** And for the rear [u-le-yarkhetei] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] westward [yammah] he made [asah] six boards [shisha qerashim].

> **Key terms:** יַרְכְתֵי (yarkhetei) = "rear/hindquarters" — from ירך ("thigh/loin"); the back wall. יָמָּה (yammah) = "seaward" — west, since the Mediterranean lies to the west of Israel. **LXX:** τὸ κλίτος... τὸ πρὸς θάλασσαν ("the side toward the sea").

**28** And two boards [u-shnei qerashim] he made [asah] for the corners [li-mqutso'ot] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] at the rear [ba-yarkhatayim].

> **Key terms:** מִקְצֹעֹת (miqutso'ot) = "corners" — from קצע ("to scrape/cut off"); the junction points where the rear wall meets the side walls. **LXX:** matches MT.

**29** And they were [ve-hayu] doubled [to'amim] from below [mi-lmattah], and together [ve-yakhdav] they were complete [yihyu tammim] to its top [al-rosho], to [el] the one ring [ha-tabba'at ha-akhat]; thus [ken] he made [asah] for both of them [li-shneihem], for the two corners [li-shnei ha-miqtso'ot].

> **Key terms:** תֹאֲמִים (to'amim) = "twinned/doubled" — from תאם ("to be twin"); the corner boards are paired. תַּמִּים (tammim) = "complete/whole" — from תמם; joined seamlessly at the top ring. **LXX:** ἐξ ἡμισείας ("from the half") — a different interpretation of the corner construction.

**30** And there were [ve-hayu] eight boards [shemonah qerashim], and their bases of silver [ve-adneihem kesef] — sixteen bases [shishah asar adanim] — two bases, two bases [shnei adanim shnei adanim] under the one board [takhat ha-qaresh ha-ekhad].

> **Key terms:** שְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים שְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים (shnei adanim shnei adanim) = "two bases, two bases" — distributive repetition emphasizing the pattern: every board, without exception, stands on two silver bases. Total: 6 rear + 2 corner = 8 boards, 16 bases. **LXX:** matches MT count.

---

## The Bars (vv. 31–34)

**31** And he made [va-ya'as] bars [berikhei] of acacia wood [atsei shittim] — five [khamishah] for the boards of the one side [le-qarshei tsela ha-mishkan ha-ekhad] of the tabernacle,

> **Key terms:** בְּרִיחֵי (berikhei) = "bars/bolts" — from ברח ("to flee/pass through"); the horizontal members that slide through rings to lock the boards together. **LXX:** μοχλούς ("bars/bolts"), matching MT.

**32** and five bars [va-khamishah verikhim] for the boards of the second side [le-qarshei tsela ha-mishkan ha-shenit] of the tabernacle, and five bars [va-khamishah verikhim] for the boards of the tabernacle [le-qarshei ha-mishkan] for the rear [la-yarkhatayim] westward [yammah].

> **Key terms:** Five bars per side: south, north, and west — fifteen bars total. **LXX:** matches MT.

**33** And he made [va-ya'as] the middle bar [et-ha-beriakh ha-tikhon] to pass through [livroakh] in the midst of the boards [be-tokh ha-qerashim] from end to end [min-ha-qatseh el-ha-qatseh].

> **Key terms:** הַבְּרִיחַ הַתִּיכֹן (ha-beriakh ha-tikhon) = "the middle bar" — the central bar runs the entire length; the other four (two upper, two lower) are shorter. לִבְרֹחַ (livroakh) = "to flee/pass through" — the bar "flees" through the boards, an evocative image. **LXX:** τὸν μοχλὸν τὸν μέσον ("the middle bolt"), matching MT.

**34** And the boards [ve-et-ha-qerashim] he overlaid [tsippah] with gold [zahav], and their rings [ve-et-tabbeotam] he made [asah] of gold [zahav] — holders [battim] for the bars [la-berikhim]; and he overlaid [va-yetsap] the bars [et-ha-berikhim] with gold [zahav].

> **Key terms:** צִפָּה (tsippah) = "he overlaid/plated" — Piel of צפה; the acacia wood is sheathed in gold. בָּתִּים (battim) = "houses/holders" — literally "houses" for the bars; the rings serve as housing through which the bars pass. **LXX:** κατεχρύσωσεν ("he gilded"), matching MT.

---

## The Veil and the Screen (vv. 35–38)

**35** And he made [va-ya'as] the veil [et-ha-parokhet] of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet of worm [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted fine linen [ve-shesh moshezar] — the work of a designer [ma'aseh khoshev] he made it [asah otah] — cherubim [keruvim].

> **Key terms:** פָּרֹכֶת (parokhet) = "veil/curtain" — the interior curtain separating the Holy Place from the Most Holy. מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב (ma'aseh khoshev) = "work of a designer" — the highest grade of weaving, with cherubim woven into the fabric (not embroidered on). **LXX:** τὸ καταπέτασμα ("the curtain"); ἔργον ὑφάντου ("work of a weaver") — slightly different craft designation.

**36** And he made [va-ya'as] for it four pillars [arba'ah ammudei] of acacia [shittim], and he overlaid them [va-yetsappem] with gold [zahav] — their hooks [vaveihem] of gold [zahav] — and he cast [va-yitsoq] for them four bases of silver [arba'ah adnei-khasef].

> **Key terms:** וָוֵיהֶם (vaveihem) = "their hooks" — from the letter-name vav, which means "hook/peg"; the hooks from which the veil hangs. וַיִּצֹק (va-yitsoq) = "and he cast/poured" — Qal of יצק; the silver bases are cast metal. **LXX:** κρίκους χρυσοῦς ("golden rings") for the hooks; ἐχώνευσεν ("he smelted") for the casting.

**37** And he made [va-ya'as] a screen [masakh] for the entrance of the tent [le-fetakh ha-ohel] of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet of worm [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted fine linen [ve-shesh moshezar] — the work of an embroiderer [ma'aseh roqem].

> **Key terms:** מָסָךְ (masakh) = "screen/hanging" — the outer entrance curtain, distinct from the inner parokhet. מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵם (ma'aseh roqem) = "work of an embroiderer" — a lower grade than the designer's work of the inner veil; the screen is embroidered (needle-worked), not woven with figures. **LXX:** ἔργον ποικιλτοῦ ("work of a variegator"), matching MT's distinction.

**38** And its pillars [ve-et-ammudav] — five [khamishah] — and their hooks [ve-et-vaveihem]; and he overlaid [ve-tsippah] their tops [rasheihem] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] with gold [zahav], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — five [khamishah] — of bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Key terms:** חֲשֻׁקֵיהֶם (khashukeihem) = "their bands/fillets" — from חשׁק ("to desire/bind"); the connecting bands encircling the pillars. Note the material gradation: the inner veil has four gold-overlaid pillars on silver bases; the outer screen has five gold-overlaid pillars on bronze bases — precious metals decrease as one moves outward from the divine Presence. **LXX:** αἱ κεφαλίδες αὐτῶν ("their capitals") for rasheihem; matches the five-pillar, bronze-base count.

---

## Manuscript Notes

**Leningrad Codex (pp. 107–108):** The text flows from the end of chapter 35 (Bezalel's appointment) into chapter 36 without a major section break. The surplus-offering narrative (vv. 1–7) stands out as unique in the Torah — nowhere else must the people be restrained from giving. The construction account (vv. 8–38) closely mirrors the instructions of chapter 26, with systematic shifts from second-person imperative to third-person narrative. The masorah marginalia are visible on both pages. A setumah (closed paragraph) break appears between v. 7 and v. 8, marking the transition from offering to construction.

**Codex Vaticanus (pp. 79–80):** The LXX dramatically rearranges Exodus 35–40. The Vaticanus manuscript pages visible in the images are headed CAP. XXVI and CAP. XXVII (chapters 26–27), confirming that what MT presents as Exodus 36 (the execution of the tabernacle construction) is relocated in the LXX to correspond with the original instruction chapters. The LXX essentially merges instruction and execution, whereas MT preserves the full command-then-fulfillment structure. This is one of the most significant MT-LXX divergences in the entire Pentateuch, and scholars debate whether the LXX reflects a shorter Hebrew Vorlage or an editorial abbreviation. The Vaticanus text on these pages covers the curtains, clasps, boards, and bars in a sequence that aligns with chapter 26's instructions rather than chapter 36's execution narrative.
